source.notes: Not an XG-disagreement claim; used to keep benchmark scoring executable while the suite grows.

decision.notes: Legal-action smoke target only; not a strategic claim.

source.title: eXtreme Gammon Search Interval article
source.notes: XG article states normal search interval may stop after the 2-ply leader; 3-ply/rollout prefer other plays and the rollout shows the blunder to be 0.085.

decision.notes: Opaque until we add XGID decoding; high-priority parser target.

title: Snake/Kauder-paradox derived position where XG allegedly misjudges side equity

source.title: Backgammon Forums: Weaknesses in eXtreme Gammon?
source.notes: Frank Berger/BGBlitz reports XG sees the straggler side as a 60-70% favorite, while he argues the snake side should be strongly favored after closure/roll-home accidents.

decision.notes: No checker dice; benchmark target is equity calibration and cube behavior, not a move choice.

title: Deep backgame 41: candidate human/BGBlitz hit on 16 versus XG 10/6

source.title: Backgammon Forums: Weaknesses in eXtreme Gammon?
source.notes: Frank Berger reports XG does not find bar/24,20/16* even after long rollout; later says Nack Ballard and Mochy/Shahab Ghodsi/Jim Pasko agreed on 20/16*.

decision.notes: Opaque until XGID decoding is implemented; source says exact truth remains hard but expert agreement makes this a priority benchmark.

title: Backgammon Boot Camp positions contradicted by XG2

source.title: The Fortuitous Press: Backgammon Boot Camp Errors
source.notes: Table lists 26 positions where XG2 contradicts older Snowie-era Boot Camp results; many are backgame/cube positions. We need board diagrams or book position extraction before these become scoreable.

position.reference: Backgammon Boot Camp error list positions 5-21, 5-25, 5-28, 5-38, 5-46, 5-48, 5-51

decision.notes: Candidate source for future suite expansion; not directly scoreable from table alone.
